Maharashtra Act No. 28) B. Copy of the Maharashtra Tenancy of Agricultural Land Rules, 2024 *
It is linked to. Accordingly, all the concerned authorities at the field level of the Revenue Department have been informed.
The following instructions are being directed to be followed in accordance with the Act and Rules: -
Legal procedure of the lessee: - Every lessee shall be liable in accordance with the AU Act, 1908.
Being registered is a must. Contracts made only orally or on plain paper will not be accepted.
It should clearly state the duration of the land and the remuneration (cash / commodity / share of production).
Ownership and possession: -
Possession of land at the expiration of the term of the contract without any legal impediment
will come to the owner. This agreement gives the lessee any permanent ownership rights in the land.
The agreement should clearly state that it will not come.
The interests of the tenant farmers: -
Crop loan from banks to the lessee farmers on the basis of registered lessee.
And the concerned banks should cooperate in getting crop insurance.

Responsibilities of the Administrator: -
Register a separate lessee in the prescribed 'form' attached by each tehsildar.
should be prepared. The register contains the name of the village, the group number, the name of the owner and lessee, and the term of the agreement.
This should be recorded accurately.
Dispute Resolution Mechanism: -
The tehsildar is competent to resolve disputes between the landlord and the lessee.
There will be authorities. It will be mandatory to give a final decision on the received application within 3 months.
The lessee may not sub-lease the land to anyone.
